mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls indicates continued labor market expansion, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS monthly increases coincide with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is held at or below 0.25%, persistent low rates suppress short-term discount rates and force investors into equities, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative more than 50% of the time over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and industrial production indicate broad-based demand expansion across consumer and manufacturing sectors, amplifying earnings growth expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both rising month-over-month
failure_condition: Instances where both RSAFS and INDPRO rise simultaneously but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of cases over a 2-year window, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion with unemployment below 5.5%, an acceleration in consumer price inflation signals overheating and impending monetary tightening, which reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.5% and CPIAUCSL accelerates month-over-month, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production indicates contracting manufacturing activity and weakening corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as markets rapidly price in the slowdown.
failure_condition: INDPRO month-over-month declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts signals strength in residential investment and broader construction activity, lifting cyclical earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST month-over-month increases coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.5% annualized confirms the economy is expanding faster than potential, supporting upward earnings revisions and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth above 2.5% are followed by negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending consumption weakness, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE rises exceeding 0.3 percentage points over 3 months are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.
